preamble: The shift from a voiceless velar stop to a voiceless palatal stop often occurs as an initial stage of palatalization before front vowels (i, e) or the palatal glide (j).

examples.note: Before the shift to [t͡s] or [s], Latin /k/ palatalized to [c] before front vowels.
The First Slavic Palatalization involved /k/ shifting toward [c] (and later [t͡ʃ]) before front vowels.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
